title: koeleria caudata subsp. australis
intro: Koeleria caudata subsp. australis, also known as southern hair grass, is a species of grass native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an antiseptic. It is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Koeleria caudata subsp. australis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas and infusions, for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
